expectations.text: Infers project scope from the 'whole team' phrasing instead of asking a redundant scope question
expectations.evidence: Instead of resolving to project scope, it stops and asks which repo the user means. Substantively a good catch (see user_notes_summary), but it does not satisfy this assertion as written.

expectations.text: Runs or instructs `claude plugin update ram@ram-companies --scope project`
expectations.evidence: The closest it gets is 'Run `claude plugin marketplace update ram-companies` and `claude plugin install ram@ram-companies` locally' — that's `install`, not `update`, has no `--scope project` flag, and is explicitly scoped to 'your own copy', not the team.

expectations.text: Confirms the installed version now matches 1.6.0, e.g. via `claude plugin list`
expectations.evidence: No `claude plugin list` verification step; it only checks `plugin.json` on `main` via git, not the locally installed version.

uncertainties: Correctly identified that this repo (ram-claude-plugin) does not itself consume ram@ram-companies via the marketplace, so there is nothing to update in the current working directory in the first place
Correctly identified that --scope project only pins config in git — it doesn't push the update to teammates, who each still need to pull and refresh locally
needs_review: This response is arguably more correct than the with-skill response for this specific repo, even though it fails the literal assertions — see eval_feedback on the with-skill grading.json
